---
title: "Composite Plane Walls — Interactive Demo"
---

$$
\newcommand{\Bi}{\mathrm{Bi}}
$$

A **composite plane wall** consists of several layers in series. Under
steady-state 1-D conduction with no heat generation, the heat flux $q''$ is
uniform across all layers and the temperature profile is **piecewise linear**
within each layer.

The total thermal resistance per unit area is

$$
R''_\text{tot} = \frac{1}{h_L} + \sum_{i=1}^{N} \frac{L_i}{k_i} + \frac{1}{h_R},
$$

and the steady heat flux is

$$
q'' = \frac{T_{\infty,L} - T_{\infty,R}}{R''_\text{tot}}.
$$

Use the demo below to build a wall of up to four layers, set boundary
conditions, and watch the temperature profile update instantly.

::: {.callout-note}
Switch between **temperature-controlled** and **heat-flux-controlled** boundary
conditions using the dropdown. In flux mode the left fluid temperature is
fixed and you prescribe $q''$ directly — useful for a known heat source such
as an electric heater or solar load on one face.
:::

---

## Interactive demo {#sec-wall-demo}

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

viewof mode = Inputs.select(
  ["Temperature BCs", "Heat flux BC"],
  { label: "Boundary condition mode", value: "Temperature BCs" }
)

viewof N_layers = Inputs.range([1, 4], {
  value: 3, step: 1, label: "Number of layers"
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Left boundary
viewof T_inf_L = Inputs.range([-50, 500], {
  value: 200, step: 1, label: "T∞ left (°C)"
})
viewof h_L = Inputs.range([1, 5000], {
  value: 100, step: 1, label: "h left (W/m²·K)"
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Right boundary
viewof T_inf_R = Inputs.range([-50, 500], {
  value: 20, step: 1, label: "T∞ right (°C)  [temperature mode only]"
})
viewof h_R = Inputs.range([1, 5000], {
  value: 25, step: 1, label: "h right (W/m²·K)"
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Imposed flux (flux mode only)
viewof q_imposed = Inputs.range([-5000, 5000], {
  value: 500, step: 10, label: "q″ imposed (W/m²)  [flux mode only, + = left→right]"
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Layer inputs
viewof L1 = Inputs.range([0.001, 0.5], { value: 0.05,  step: 0.001, label: "Layer 1 thickness (m)" })
viewof k1 = Inputs.range([0.01,  400], { value: 1.0,   step: 0.01,  label: "Layer 1 k (W/m·K)" })
viewof L2 = Inputs.range([0.001, 0.5], { value: 0.10,  step: 0.001, label: "Layer 2 thickness (m)" })
viewof k2 = Inputs.range([0.01,  400], { value: 50.0,  step: 0.1,   label: "Layer 2 k (W/m·K)" })
viewof L3 = Inputs.range([0.001, 0.5], { value: 0.02,  step: 0.001, label: "Layer 3 thickness (m)" })
viewof k3 = Inputs.range([0.01,  400], { value: 0.04,  step: 0.001, label: "Layer 3 k (W/m·K)" })
viewof L4 = Inputs.range([0.001, 0.5], { value: 0.05,  step: 0.001, label: "Layer 4 thickness (m)" })
viewof k4 = Inputs.range([0.01,  400], { value: 15.0,  step: 0.1,   label: "Layer 4 k (W/m·K)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

// ── All physics in one block so every derived variable is in the same scope ───

all_layers = [
  {L: L1, k: k1},
  {L: L2, k: k2},
  {L: L3, k: k3},
  {L: L4, k: k4},
]

layers = all_layers.slice(0, N_layers)

R_conv_L = 1 / h_L
R_conv_R = 1 / h_R
R_cond   = layers.reduce((s, lay) => s + lay.L / lay.k, 0)
R_tot    = R_conv_L + R_cond + R_conv_R

q_flux = mode === "Temperature BCs"
  ? (T_inf_L - T_inf_R) / R_tot
  : q_imposed

// Walk temperatures left to right across interfaces
T_surf_L = T_inf_L - q_flux * R_conv_L

interface_xs = (() => {
  let xs = [0]
  let x = 0
  for (let i = 0; i < layers.length; i++) {
    x += layers[i].L
    xs.push(x)
  }
  return xs
})()

interface_Ts = (() => {
  let Ts = [T_surf_L]
  let T = T_surf_L
  for (let i = 0; i < layers.length; i++) {
    T = T - q_flux * layers[i].L / layers[i].k
    Ts.push(T)
  }
  return Ts
})()

x_total  = interface_xs[interface_xs.length - 1]
T_surf_R = interface_Ts[interface_Ts.length - 1]
T_fluid_R_actual = mode === "Temperature BCs"
  ? T_inf_R
  : T_surf_R + q_flux * R_conv_R

x_pad = x_total * 0.10

// Points for the temperature profile line including fluid anchors
line_pts = [
  {x: -x_pad, T: T_inf_L},
  ...interface_xs.map((x, i) => ({x, T: interface_Ts[i]})),
  {x: x_total + x_pad, T: T_fluid_R_actual}
]

wall_pts = interface_xs.map((x, i) => ({x, T: interface_Ts[i]}))

fluid_pts = [
  {x: -x_pad,          T: T_inf_L,          label: "T∞,L = " + T_inf_L.toFixed(1) + "°C"},
  {x: x_total + x_pad, T: T_fluid_R_actual,  label: "T∞,R = " + T_fluid_R_actual.toFixed(1) + "°C"},
]

layer_colors = ["#ddeeff", "#fff5d6", "#e8f5e8", "#fde8f0"]

layer_rects = layers.map((lay, i) => ({
  x1: interface_xs[i],
  x2: interface_xs[i + 1],
  fill: layer_colors[i],
  label: "k = " + lay.k.toFixed(2) + " W/m·K",
  R: (lay.L / lay.k).toFixed(4)
}))

T_min = Math.min(T_inf_L, T_fluid_R_actual, ...interface_Ts)
T_max = Math.max(T_inf_L, T_fluid_R_actual, ...interface_Ts)
T_span = T_max - T_min < 1 ? 10 : T_max - T_min
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

// ── Summary row ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
html`<div style="display:flex; gap:28px; flex-wrap:wrap; margin:0.4em 0 1em; font-size:0.93em; font-family:Georgia,serif;">
  <div><strong>q″ =</strong> ${q_flux.toFixed(1)} W/m²</div>
  <div><strong>R″_tot =</strong> ${R_tot.toFixed(4)} m²·K/W</div>
  <div><strong>T_s,L =</strong> ${T_surf_L.toFixed(1)} °C</div>
  <div><strong>T_s,R =</strong> ${T_surf_R.toFixed(1)} °C</div>
</div>`
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

// ── Temperature profile plot ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
Plot.plot({
  width: 660,
  height: 390,
  marginLeft: 68,
  marginBottom: 52,
  marginRight: 24,
  marginTop: 36,
  style: { fontFamily: "Georgia, serif", fontSize: "13px" },
  x: {
    label: "Position x (m) →",
    domain: [-x_pad * 1.5, x_total + x_pad * 1.5],
  },
  y: {
    label: "↑ Temperature (°C)",
    domain: [T_min - T_span * 0.18, T_max + T_span * 0.22],
  },
  marks: [
    // layer shading
    ...layer_rects.map(r =>
      Plot.rect([r], {
        x1: "x1", x2: "x2",
        y1: T_min - T_span * 0.18,
        y2: T_max + T_span * 0.22,
        fill: "fill",
        stroke: "#bbb",
        strokeWidth: 0.5,
      })
    ),

    // layer k labels
    Plot.text(layer_rects, {
      x: r => (r.x1 + r.x2) / 2,
      y: T_max + T_span * 0.14,
      text: "label",
      fontSize: 11,
      fill: "#555",
      textAnchor: "middle",
    }),

    // wall-surface boundary lines
    Plot.ruleX([0, x_total], {
      stroke: "#999", strokeWidth: 1, strokeDasharray: "5,3"
    }),

    Plot.gridY({ stroke: "#eee" }),

    // temperature profile
    Plot.line(line_pts, {
      x: "x", y: "T",
      stroke: "#c0392b",
      strokeWidth: 2.5,
    }),

    // wall interface dots
    Plot.dot(wall_pts, {
      x: "x", y: "T",
      r: 5, fill: "#c0392b",
    }),

    // fluid anchor circles (open)
    Plot.dot(fluid_pts, {
      x: "x", y: "T",
      r: 6, fill: "white", stroke: "#c0392b", strokeWidth: 2,
    }),

    // fluid temperature labels
    Plot.text(fluid_pts, {
      x: "x", y: "T",
      text: "label",
      dy: -14,
      fontSize: 11,
      fill: "#c0392b",
      textAnchor: d => d.x < 0 ? "start" : "end",
    }),
  ]
})
```

---

## Resistance network {#sec-resistance}

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

// ── Resistance network SVG ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
{
  const boxes = []
  boxes.push({
    label: "1/h_L",
    sub: R_conv_L.toFixed(4),
    color: "#d6eaf8"
  })
  layers.forEach((lay, i) => {
    boxes.push({
      label: "L" + (i+1) + "/k" + (i+1),
      sub: (lay.L / lay.k).toFixed(4),
      color: layer_colors[i]
    })
  })
  boxes.push({
    label: "1/h_R",
    sub: R_conv_R.toFixed(4),
    color: "#d6eaf8"
  })

  const BW = 82, gap = 24, BH = 46, pad = 36
  const total_w = boxes.length * BW + (boxes.length - 1) * gap + 2 * pad
  const cy = 52

  let svg = `<svg viewBox="0 0 ${total_w} ${cy + 52}" style="width:100%;max-width:660px;overflow:visible;" aria-label="Thermal resistance network">`

  // left wire
  svg += `<line x1="0" y1="${cy}" x2="${pad}" y2="${cy}" stroke="#555" stroke-width="1.5"/>`

  boxes.forEach((b, i) => {
    const x = pad + i * (BW + gap)
    if (i > 0) {
      svg += `<line x1="${x}" y1="${cy}" x2="${x}" y2="${cy}" stroke="#555" stroke-width="1.5"/>`
      svg += `<line x1="${x - gap}" y1="${cy}" x2="${x}" y2="${cy}" stroke="#555" stroke-width="1.5"/>`
    }
    svg += `<rect x="${x}" y="${cy - BH/2}" width="${BW}" height="${BH}" rx="4" fill="${b.color}" stroke="#aaa" stroke-width="0.8"/>`
    svg += `<text x="${x + BW/2}" y="${cy - 8}" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" font-family="Georgia,serif" fill="#333">${b.label}</text>`
    svg += `<text x="${x + BW/2}" y="${cy + 8}" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" font-family="Georgia,serif" fill="#555">${b.sub}</text>`
    svg += `<text x="${x + BW/2}" y="${cy + 20}" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" font-family="Georgia,serif" fill="#888">m²·K/W</text>`
  })

  // right wire
  const x_end = pad + boxes.length * BW + (boxes.length - 1) * gap
  svg += `<line x1="${x_end}" y1="${cy}" x2="${x_end + pad}" y2="${cy}" stroke="#555" stroke-width="1.5"/>`

  // T labels at ends
  svg += `<text x="4" y="${cy + 5}" font-size="12" font-family="Georgia,serif" fill="#c0392b">T∞,L</text>`
  svg += `<text x="${x_end + pad - 2}" y="${cy + 5}" font-size="12" font-family="Georgia,serif" fill="#c0392b" text-anchor="end">T∞,R</text>`

  // R_tot below
  svg += `<text x="${total_w/2}" y="${cy + 42}" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" font-family="Georgia,serif" fill="#333">R″_tot = ${R_tot.toFixed(4)} m²·K/W</text>`

  svg += `</svg>`
  return html`${svg}`
}
```

---

## What to look for {#sec-observations}

**Observation 1 — Steepest slope = highest resistance.**
The temperature drops most steeply across the layer with the largest $L/k$.
Try setting one layer to $k = 0.04$ W/m·K (typical insulation) and watch
nearly all the temperature drop concentrate there.

**Observation 2 — Convection resistance can dominate.**
Reduce $h_L$ to 5 W/m²·K (still-air natural convection). You will likely find
$1/h_L$ dwarfs all the conduction resistances combined — a reminder that
adding more insulation has diminishing returns when convection is the
bottleneck.

**Observation 3 — Flux mode decouples the two sides.**
Switch to heat flux mode and set $q'' = 1000$ W/m². The right-side fluid
temperature is no longer an input but a result determined by $R_{\text{conv},R}$.
This models a wall with a known radiative or electrical heat source on one face.

**Observation 4 — Series resistance is additive.**
Double any single layer thickness and $R''_\text{tot}$ increases by exactly
$\Delta L / k$ — visible immediately in the resistance network boxes below
the plot.
